California
Fair POlitical
Practices Comrnission
Septenber 25, L990
David R. Tooley
City Manager
City of Eureka
531- K Street
Eureka, CA 9 5501-116 5
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-9 0-5 4 5
Dear Mr. Tooley:
Thi,s is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behal,f of Lance lladsen, Eureka City Councilmember, concerning his
duties under the conf I ict-of - interest provj-sions of the political
Ref orm Act (the trActrr ) . J'
OUESTION PRESENTED
Does Mr. Madsen, a forner city enployee, have a conflict-of-
interest when he is 1egaI1y entitled to compensation for sick
leave time accumulated vhile he was a city employee and there is
no related governmental decision pending before the city council?
CONCLUSION
Mr. Madsen does not have a confL ict-of- interest under the Act
when he is 1ega11y entitled to compensation for sick leave time
accurnulated while he was a city ernployee or when there is no
related governmental decision pending before the city council.
1 
Got ".nment code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated'
Conmission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18ooo; et seo. AI1 references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, suite 8oo o P.o. Box 8O7 o sacramento cA 958O4'O8O7 o (916)322'5660


Our Fi‚ñÝe NO. A-90-545
Page 2
FACTS2
Lance Madsen has been recently been elected to the Eureka
City Council and is due to take office on November 20, L99O. !tr.
Madsen is also a former city enployee who retired due to an
industrial disability. 
According to your analysis, under
covernment Code Section 21025.2 an employer is required to
compensate an employee for accurnulated sick leave time if the
enployee retires due to industrial disability. 
Thus under your
analysis Mr. Madsen is Iegal1y entitled to compensation for his
accumulated sick leave tine, although the city has not yet paid
such conpensation to hirn. Paynent of such compensation lrould be
handled adrninistratively and would not require any action by the
city council.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 sets forth the confl ict-of-interest 
prohibition
of the Act:
No public official 
at any level of state or
Ioca1 government shall make, participate in rnaking
or j,n any way attempt to use his official 
position
to influence a governmental decision in which he
kno$rs or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.
As set forth
make, participate
official position
ÔΩé
„ÄÄ
ÔΩé
Ôºê
„Éª
‚ñÝ
„Éª
‚ñÝ
ÔΩî
Section 87‚ñÝ00, a public Official shall not
making or in any way attempt to use his
inf‚ñÝuence a governmental decision.  From the
facts presented to us Mr. Madsen does not appear to be making,
participating in making or in any way using his position to
influence a lrovernmental decision. No decision related to the
payment of Mr. ltadsen's benefits is before the city council-.
Indeed the payrnent of the benefits to !1r. Madsen will be handled
at an adninistrative leveI.
Iiloreover, for there to be a conflict of interest there must
be a financial. interest involved. "Financial interesttt is defined
by Section 87 7O3:.
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it j.s
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have
a uraterial financial effect, distinguishabLe from
its effect on the public generally, on the official
or a member of his or he irnmediate family or on:
2 this advice is based on the facts presented in your letter of
August 13, L99O, our tefephone conversation of Auqust 23, L99o,
and ny telephone conversation with David W. Tyson, Dj-rector of
Finance, on SePtember 6, 1,990.


Our Fi‚ñÝe No. A-90-545
Page 3
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,0o0) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest lrorth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or rnore.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terEs available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty 
dol,-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
nonths prior to the tirne when the decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, offj-cer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of managenent.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, recej-ved by, or promised to the public
official within 12 nonths prior to the tirne when
the decision is made.
(section 87103 (a) - (e) . )
Under the facts presented to us, Mr. Iladsen does not have a
ttfinancial interestrr as defined by the Act. salaries from locaI
governmental agencies are specj,ficalJ,y excluded from the
definition of income under the Act. 
(Section 82030(b) (2);
Regulation 18702.1(c) (1).) 
Fringe benefits that are part of a
cornpensation package paid by a government agency are included qrith
the term rrsalaryrr in Section 82030(b) (2). 
(In re lloore, (L977) 3
FPPC Ops. 33, 36i James Advice Letter, A-88-459; copies enclosed-)
While Mr. Madsen may have an interest in compensation for his
accumulated sick leave tirne from the city, this interest is
excluded from the def j.nition of 'rincome,rt and therefore Mr. Madsen
does not have a "financial interestrr under Secti'on 87103. None of
the other specified financial interests in Section 87103 appear
involved here.
Mr. Madsen is not naking, participating in makinq or in any
way using his official 
position-to influence a governmentaf
aelisionl 
Nor does he have a financiat interest involved here.
Mr. lladsen has no conf l ict-of-interest.


' 
our File No. A-90-545
Paqe 4
If you should have any question regarding this natter, please
feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-590L.
s incere Iy,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
